title: artemisia vulgaris subsp. inundata
intro: Artemisia vulgaris subsp. inundata, also known as water mugwort, is a subspecies of the mugwort plant (Artemisia vulgaris) that is native to wetland areas and grows in shallow water. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a natural antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic, due to the presence of compounds like thujone and sesquiterpenes. The health benefits of water mugwort include reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, water mugwort can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to create natural remedies for skin issues, menstrual cramps, and respiratory problems.
